title: in vitro assembly and transformation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5v9mp","text":"For the in vitro assembly of the DNA fragments, we decided to use the NEBuilder®, an assembly kit based on the Gibson Assembly. In NEBuilder®, exonucleases break down the ends of the DNA fragments and hybridize the cohesive ends, followed by DNA polymerases that synthesize the broken strands. Finally, DNA ligase repairs the nick and completes the DNA assembly. it is expected that in vitro assembly will reliably transform the cyclized plasmid.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":79,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1dfti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cku2i","text":"For the subsequent transformation, we used the NEB® 10-beta competent cell because NEB® 10-beta can be largely maintained the cloning efficiency even with high molecular weight plasmids, which we considered to be an advantage over other strains when transforming our team's plasmids.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":50,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":86,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dr6ro","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c2bga","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"53afs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"61j79","text":"Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8sjtp","text":"DNA samples","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvqt2","text":"Cas12a fragment 1","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7a06m","text":"Cas12a fragment 2","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"950ec","text":"Cas12a fragment 3","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e37ef","text":"pSB1A3","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2m0gh","text":"NEBuilder® Assembly Master Mix (New England Biolabs)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":9,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bce6q","text":"NEB® 10-beta competent cell (New England Biolabs)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":3,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7k07","text":"SOC medium","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ig21","text":"LB agar plate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: DNA solutions and reagents were mixed according to the compositions in the table below. A B C 1 COMPONENT VOLUME(µl) CONCENTRATION 2 NEBuilder Assembly Master Mix 10.0 ×2 3 Cas12a fragment 1 0.75 0.34 pmol 4 Cas12a fragment 2 0.90 0.35 pmol 5 Cas12a fragment 3 1.00 0.33 pmol 6 pSB1A3 0.60 0.36 pmol 7 Nuclease Free water 6.75 8 Total Volume 20.0 �
Thaw the DNA solution and NEBuilder ® Assembly Master Mix on ice.
Mix the DNA solution by vortexing, and centrifuge to collect the solution to the bottom of the tube.
Thaw Escherichia coli NEB ® 10-beta competent cell on ice.
Add 2 μl assembled sample into competent cell tube.
Heat shock at 42℃ for 30 seconds on heat block.
Add 950 μl of SOC medium and mix gently pipetting.
Incubate the tube at 37℃ for 1 hour.
Centrifuge at 5,000 g for 1 minute at room temperature
Resuspend cells with the remaining supernatant by Voltex
Spread the whole culture on agar plates containing ampicillin.
creator: Yuichiroh Ikagawa
